mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand outpaces productive capacity, generating inflationary pressure that forces equity repricing and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint Rank IC of INDPRO and RSAFS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not systematically decline when both series move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained drop in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader demand contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >50% of observations, breaking the leading indicator relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.0% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real yields and prompts equity risk premium expansion, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL breaches the 265.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust corporate revenue generation, supporting earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth months are consistently followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising real estate activity and tightening monetary policy creates a liquidity crunch for highly leveraged corporate borrowers, increasing default risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of HOUST and FEDFUNDS shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day SPX returns, or forward returns are not lower when both series move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates beyond 3.0% annualized, supply constraints in industrial output become binding, triggering margin compression and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not systematically negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds the 3.0% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.
